seo.title: Construction coordination platform
seo.description: EdilSync centralizes communication and planning for contractors, owners, and subcontractors.
seo.softwareDescription: Construction software to centralize communication, planning, and traceable execution.

hero.subtitle: EdilSync brings owners, contractors, subcontractors, and professionals into one platform. Fewer calls, fewer misunderstandings, more control.
hero.note: Personal or company account · No card required to start · Upgrade when needed

highlights.alignmentText: One place for tasks, photos, change requests, and worksite conversations.
highlights.traceabilityText: Every action becomes usable evidence for decisions, work progress, and disputes.
highlights.controlText: Fewer clarification calls, fewer wasted trips, fewer gray areas.

preview.feedText: The owner sees progress and decisions. The contractor keeps context without chasing chats and attachments.

stats.aLabel: Lost every year because of poor communication
stats.bLabel: Of worksites miss expected quality standards
stats.cLabel: Lost weekly to conflicts and rework
stats.dLabel: Of rework tied to inadequate communication

problem.title: Construction communication is chaotic
problem.subtitle: Every day, different tools are used to run construction work, but the result is misalignment and communication outside the right context.
problem.c1d: Information gets lost across private chats, groups, and endless threads. Finding that important conversation from 3 weeks ago becomes impossible.
problem.c2d: The owner calls for updates. Subcontractors call to coordinate. Every interruption costs about 15 minutes of productivity.
problem.c3d: Undocumented changes turn into disputes. With 3-5% margins, a single misunderstanding can wipe out the profit of the whole worksite.
problem.c4d: Who caused the delay? The supplier? The client who did not choose materials? Without documentation, blame always falls on the contractor.
problem.c5d: The electrician arrives but the previous work is not finished. Every wasted trip costs $100-200, multiplied dozens of times each year.
problem.c6d: The owner does not know what is happening, feels anxious, and keeps calling. Not out of mistrust, but because no tool gives real transparency.

solution.title: One shared space for every stakeholder
solution.subtitle: A contextual platform where roles, permissions, and responsibilities adapt by worksite and workflow.
solution.s1d: The contractor creates the site and invites owners, subcontractors, and professionals in one click.
solution.s2d: Every action - photos, tasks, changes, messages - automatically becomes traceable documentation.
solution.s3d: Everyone sees the same worksite state. Zero ambiguity.

result.label: Operational result
result.title: Everyone sees the same site without chasing updates.
result.b1: The owner follows progress and decisions without constant phone calls.
result.b2: The contractor coordinates tasks, photo evidence, and change requests in the same flow.
result.b3: The subcontractor sees only what is needed, when it is needed.

features.title: Everything your site needs
features.subtitle: Tasks, photos, changes, chat, and financial control in one integrated flow.
features.f1d: Organize work by rooms, phases, and milestones. Blocked status with tagged ownership to document who causes delays.
features.f2d: Captured photos automatically become part of the worksite. Timestamp, task-based organization, and built-in legal protection.
features.f3d: Every change is tracked with cost and timeline impact. The owner approves or rejects. No more disputes like 'I thought it was included'.
features.f4d: Worksite messages with references to tasks, milestones, and documents. Replaces WhatsApp while keeping everything in the right context.
features.f5d: Coordinate visits, teams, and deadlines with conflict detection.
features.f6d: Formalize disputes with timeline, evidence, comments, and states.
features.f7d: Budget, costs, rates, and work progress statements. The owner sees progress while the contractor manages financial control.
features.f8d: GPS optional clock-in/out linked to worksite costs.

why.title: Not another generic management tool
why.w1d: The only tool built around contractor protection. Automatic documentation protects both sides without extra work.
why.w2d: Designed for the jobsite, not the office desk. Upload photos in 3 taps, update status in 3 taps. If mobile has friction, it fails.
why.w3d: If it takes more than 5 minutes to understand, we did it wrong. Clear interfaces, no hidden functionality, same model for every role.
why.w4d: You do not need to 'write updates'. Your actions - photos, task updates, approvals - automatically become the worksite feed.

audience.title: Designed for every role on site
audience.subtitle: Same tool, role-adapted interface. No one needs to learn from scratch.

faqs.q: Does the homeowner need to pay?
faqs.a: No. Homeowners remain free. Subcontractors and professionals can also join invited worksites for free.
Yes, no penalties and no hidden costs. Cancel any time from settings.

faqs.q: What happens to data if I cancel?
faqs.a: Data stays in the system, but company and worksite premium areas become inaccessible until a paid plan or valid sponsorship is restored.
No. EdilSync is month-to-month and you can cancel any time.

faqs.q: Do you offer an enterprise plan?
faqs.a: We are preparing an Enterprise plan. Write to info@rdlabs.digital to discuss your needs.

featuresPage.seoDescription: All EdilSync features for worksite coordination, communication, documentation, field operations, and dispute protection, with a clear split between company premium and sponsored-worksite premium.
featuresPage.title: Everything in one platform
featuresPage.subtitle: Not separate modules, but one connected ecosystem covering every layer of construction coordination from first photo to final progress statement.
featuresPage.note: Private owners and invited collaborators remain free. Company premium belongs to the Pro company; milestones, financial control, advanced documents, and full worksite chat unlock on sponsored worksites.

sections.title: Worksite Management
sections.note: The premium worksite surfaces described here unlock on sponsored worksites.

cards.title: Tasks and Milestones
cards.description: Organize execution by phases, rooms, and milestones. Each task has status, due date, owner, and optional blocked accountability. Board and list views included.

cards.title: Change Requests / Variations
cards.description: Every requested change is tracked with cost and time impact. Owners can approve, reject, or ask for clarifications. Fully time-stamped.

cards.title: Shared Calendar
cards.description: Unified calendar for events, site visits, and due tasks. Detects conflicts automatically and notifies all involved participants.

sections.title: Communication
sections.note: Chat and notifications stay contextual; advanced worksite references expand on sponsored worksites.

cards.title: Contextual Chat
cards.description: Worksite and company messages linked to tasks, milestones, documents, and change requests through structured clickable references.

cards.title: Smart Notifications
cards.description: In-app and email notifications for key events: invitations, assignments, status updates, and mentions. Configurable by type and channel.

cards.title: Invites and Participation
cards.description: Invite owners, subcontractors, and professionals by email. Track invite status and apply contextual permissions automatically.

sections.title: Documentation
sections.note: Advanced document workflows and full technical collaboration are strongest on sponsored worksites.

cards.title: Photo Documentation
cards.description: Photos are automatically linked to the worksite, time-stamped, and organized by task and area with built-in legal protection.

cards.title: Advanced Document Management
cards.description: Upload, preview, download, comments, revisions, and categorization with advanced metadata for discipline, phase, and status.

cards.title: BIM and 3D Viewer
cards.description: On sponsored worksites, native support for IFC, GLB, and GLTF with in-app orbit controls, IFC parsing, and automatic fallback to external viewer when needed.

sections.title: Finance and Operations
sections.note: Company attendance belongs to the Pro company; worksite financial control and worksite operations depend on active sponsorship.

cards.title: Financial Control
cards.description: Budgets, costs, labor rates, and progress statements with role-based visibility on forecasts, approved extras, and contested items.

cards.title: Attendance and Clock-in
cards.description: Clock-in/out with optional GPS, entry and exit notes, worksite linkage, data export, and integration with cost control.

cards.title: Dashboards and Analytics
cards.description: Role-aware dashboards showing progress indicators, completed tasks, active worksites, and team presence across contexts.

cards.title: Dispute Management
cards.description: Formalize conflicts on scope, costs, delays, quality, and payments with event timelines, evidence, and resolution notes.

cards.title: Contextual Permissions
cards.description: Permission levels across app scope, active context, company role, and worksite role. Fully relational and traceable.

cards.title: Automatic Documentation
cards.description: Daily actions become documentation automatically: status changes, uploads, approvals, and messages are captured in the worksite feed.

sections.title: Usage Modes
sections.note: Operative mode follows the same permissions and entitlement logic as the full workspace.

cards.title: Normal workspace
cards.description: Full workspace with dashboards, worksites, calendar, companies, notifications, and settings for advanced management.

cards.title: Operative Mode
cards.description: Mobile-first interface for field teams with quick clock-in/out, daily tasks, photo uploads, and compact chat.

cards.title: Contextual role access
cards.description: The same platform adapts visibility and actions based on active context, company role, and worksite role without duplicating surfaces.

faqPage.seoDescription: Frequently asked questions about EdilSync: platform, pricing, capabilities, permissions, security, and support.
faqPage.title: Frequently asked questions
faqPage.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about EdilSync.

items.a: EdilSync is a SaaS platform for construction-site and renovation coordination. It brings together clients, contractors, subcontractors, and professionals in one shared workspace, with contextual roles and permissions adapted to each worksite.

items.q: Is EdilSync suitable for small companies?
items.a: Absolutely. EdilSync is designed specifically for construction companies under €1M annual revenue that manage 8-12 residential renovations. The interface is simple, mobile-first, and does not require training.
EdilSync is available in Italian and English. You can switch language at any time from the user menu.
EdilSync Pro for companies costs €19/month or €190/year. Private owners remain free. Companies can stay free or upgrade to Pro to unlock worksite sponsorship and company premium capabilities.

items.q: Can I get started without a card?
items.a: There is no separate trial tier. You can get started without a card using a personal account or a free company, then upgrade to Pro when you need sponsorship or company premium.
Yes, you can cancel your subscription at any time directly from settings. There are no penalties and no hidden costs.

items.q: Do my owners need to pay?
items.a: No. The homeowner remains free. Subcontractors and professionals can also join invited worksites for free. Premium worksite capabilities depend on an active sponsorship from a paid company.

items.q: Can I use EdilSync from the jobsite on phone?
items.a: Yes, EdilSync is mobile-first. There is a dedicated operative mode for on-site use, designed for workers, site managers, and anyone working in the field. Photo uploads, clock-in/out, and task updates happen in just a few taps.

items.q: How do permissions work? Can everyone see everything?
items.a: No. EdilSync uses a 4-level contextual permission model: application role, active context (personal or company), company role, and worksite role. Each person only sees what is relevant to their role in that specific worksite.
Yes. EdilSync natively supports multi-level subcontracting. A contractor can invite subcontractors, who can in turn involve their own teams. Permissions and access are controlled at every level.

items.q: Can I view BIM files or 3D models?
items.a: Yes, but as a premium worksite capability. On sponsored worksites, EdilSync supports IFC, GLB, and GLTF with an in-app IFC viewer and a 3D viewer for GLB/GLTF. On unsponsored worksites, these files cannot be uploaded and existing ones stay archived without preview.

The disputes module lets you formalize conflicts about scope, costs, delays, quality, or payments. Each dispute has an event timeline and can include evidence, comments, resolution notes, and stakeholder notifications. Everything is documented and immutable.

Yes. EdilSync runs on cloud infrastructure with Row Level Security (RLS) enforced on every data operation. Data access is built on synchronized user sets automatically derived from memberships and worksite participation.
Only people you have invited as worksite participants. Each user only sees worksites where they are an active participant, and only the sections appropriate for their role in that worksite.

faqPage.finalTitle: Ready to bring order to your construction site?
faqPage.finalText: Stop wasting time across chats, emails, and calls. Start with EdilSync today and feel the difference from day one.
faqPage.finalNote: Personal or company account - No card required to start - Upgrade when needed

howItWorksPage.seoDescription: From zero to a coordinated construction site in minutes: onboarding, worksite setup, participant invites, daily execution, and final control.
howItWorksPage.title: From zero to a coordinated jobsite in minutes
howItWorksPage.subtitle: EdilSync is designed to work from day one, with no heavy training and no complex setup.

steps.title: Create your account
steps.text: Sign up in 2 minutes. No card required to get started. Choose whether to operate as an individual or company.
steps.note: Each person has one account and can operate in multiple contexts - as owner, company member, or professional. One login for everything.

steps.title: Create your company
steps.text: Register your contractor company in EdilSync. Add collaborators, assign operational roles, and activate your internal communication channel.
steps.note: The company is the operational unit of the jobsite. You can have site managers, foremen, workers, and backoffice with contextual permissions.

steps.title: Create the worksite
steps.text: Open a new worksite in EdilSync. Add address, dates, description, and initial photo documentation.
steps.note: The worksite is the container for all relationships: owner, contractor, subcontractors, professionals, docs, tasks, chat, and finance.

steps.title: Invite participants
steps.text: Invite owners, subcontractors, and professionals by email. Each participant sees only relevant scope with role-based permissions.
steps.note: The homeowner joins free. Subcontractors and professionals can join invited worksites for free. Premium worksite capabilities unlock when a paid company sponsors the worksite.

steps.title: Work and document
steps.text: Tasks, photos, messages, changes, deadlines - every action automatically becomes traceable worksite documentation.
steps.note: You do not need to write manual updates. Daily actions become the jobsite journal, visible in real time.

steps.title: Control and close
steps.text: Monitor progress, costs, and milestones. Manage approved changes and documented disputes. Close with complete worksite history.
steps.note: From field attendance to cost control, everything remains connected. Final progress statement includes full evidence in one place.

howItWorksPage.finalTitle: Ready to bring order to your construction site?
howItWorksPage.finalText: Stop wasting time across chats, emails, and calls. Start with EdilSync today and feel the difference from day one.
howItWorksPage.finalNote: Personal or company account · 2-minute setup · Upgrade when needed

disputeProtectionPage.seoDescription: Reduce jobsite disputes with contextual proof, traceable change requests, and structured communication in one workspace.
disputeProtectionPage.title: Every word is written.
disputeProtectionPage.subtitle: In construction, margins are often 3-5%. One undocumented dispute can erase profit on an entire worksite. EdilSync protects you automatically, without extra admin work.
disputeProtectionPage.note: Built for contractors, owners, subcontractors, and technical professionals.
disputeProtectionPage.scenarioQuote: "I lost €4,200 on a €28,000 worksite because of a dispute on two rooms the client said were already included. I had nothing documented. Only my word."
disputeProtectionPage.issuesTitle: The most common disputes - and how you prevent them
disputeProtectionPage.issuesSubtitle: Every painful scenario has a clear documentary response.

issues.title: "I thought it was included in the quote"
issues.text: Change requests document every variation with cost and schedule impact. The owner approves or rejects. Everything is time-stamped and immutable.

issues.title: "I did not know the delay was on me"
issues.text: Blocked tasks register owner, date, and reason. If delay depends on a client decision, it is clearly recorded.

issues.title: "That photo proves nothing"
issues.text: Photos are time-stamped and linked to the specific task. Initial and final state of each area is documented automatically.

issues.title: "But I told you on the phone"
issues.text: All key communication lives in-app with structured references. No critical decisions are buried in private chats or email.

disputeProtectionPage.flowSubtitle: It is not an extra form to fill. It is the result of your daily operations.

flowSteps.title: Your actions become evidence
flowSteps.text: Every photo, task update, and approval is automatically recorded with timestamp in the worksite feed. No extra effort required.

flowSteps.title: Variations are formalized
flowSteps.text: When scope changes, a change request captures description, time impact, and cost impact. Approval is digital and immutable.

flowSteps.title: Delays have named responsibility
flowSteps.text: Tasks can be marked blocked with cause and accountable party. Waiting on a client decision is documented with date and time.

flowSteps.title: Disputes are handled formally
flowSteps.text: If conflict starts, open a formal dispute with event timeline, attached evidence, and comments. Everything in one place, fully traceable.

disputeProtectionPage.roiTitle: One avoided dispute is worth months of subscription
disputeProtectionPage.roiText: An average construction dispute can cost between €200 and €2,000 in time, rework, and lost margin. EdilSync costs €19 per month. ROI is straightforward.

roiCards.label: Average cost of a minor dispute
ROI with one avoided dispute per year

disputeProtectionPage.finalTitle: Ready to bring order to your construction site?
disputeProtectionPage.finalText: Stop wasting time between chats, emails, and phone calls. Start with EdilSync today and feel the difference from day one.
disputeProtectionPage.finalNote: Free or Pro company - No card required to start - Upgrade when needed

transparencyPage.seoDescription: Follow your worksite in real time without constant calls: live feed, meaningful alerts and, on sponsored worksites, clear milestones and visibility on costs and changes.
transparencyPage.title: Always know what is happening.
transparencyPage.subtitle: You are investing serious money in your home or property. You get real-time visibility on the jobsite and, when the worksite is sponsored, access to premium coordination and financial transparency areas.
transparencyPage.note: Homeowners always stay free. Milestones, approved costs, and structured chat unlock on sponsored worksites.
transparencyPage.quote: "I spent €65,000 to renovate my apartment. For three months I called the contractor every two days just to understand progress. I felt annoying, but I had no alternative."
compareRows: You call the contractor to ask for updates
Open the app and see the updated feed
You do not know if a change will increase final cost
Every change includes cost and schedule impact
Delay is blamed on you even when material was pending
You cannot understand the progress report you received
Progress status is visible in real time
You do surprise site visits because you do not trust updates
You get full visibility without disturbing anyone
transparencyPage.featuresTitle: Everything you can see as a homeowner
transparencyPage.featuresSubtitle: Immediate worksite visibility, with premium areas enabled when a Pro company sponsors the worksite.

features.title: Real-time worksite feed
features.text: Every action - photo uploaded, task completed, change approved - appears in the feed. You see the same worksite reality as the contractor.

features.title: Proactive alerts
features.text: When something relevant happens, you get notified. No more anxiety wondering what is happening on site.

features.title: Photo documentation
features.text: Site photos become automatically visible in the worksite. Track each area day by day without unscheduled inspections.

features.title: Clear milestones and deadlines
features.text: On sponsored worksites, the shared timeline includes clear milestones and deadlines. You know what to expect and receive alerts if something slips, with documented cause.

features.title: Financial transparency
features.text: On sponsored worksites, you can see budget, approved costs, and progress status. Scope changes arrive as formal change requests with explicit impact.

features.title: Direct worksite channel
features.text: On sponsored worksites, worksite chat keeps communication in context, linked to tasks and documents. No more lost emails or scattered messages.

transparencyPage.finalTitle: Ready to bring order to your construction site?
transparencyPage.finalText: Stop wasting time across chats, emails, and calls. Start with EdilSync today and see the difference from day one.
transparencyPage.finalNote: Guided access · No card required to start · Fast collaboration

teamCoordinationPage.seoDescription: Align contractors, owners, subcontractors, and technical professionals in one workflow with shared tasks, calendar, and contextual communication.
teamCoordinationPage.title: A jobsite has 4-8 actors.
teamCoordinationPage.subtitle: Contractor, owner, subcontractors, architects. Each has different priorities, schedules, and tools. EdilSync aligns everyone in one shared workspace.

stakeholders.text: Manages the worksite, assigns tasks, approves changes, monitors costs.
Real-time visibility, approves change requests, communicates with contractor.
Sees own tasks, shared calendar, and contractor updates.
Handles technical docs and coordinates with contractor and owner.

problems.title: Double-booked work areas
problems.text: Electrician and tiler are scheduled in the same bathroom on the same day. Nobody knew.

problems.title: Subcontractor arrives with no prep complete
problems.text: Preparatory work is not finished. The plumber drove 60km for nothing. Nobody warned them.

problems.title: Blocking decision not escalated
problems.text: Client must choose tiles. Work stops. Contractor does not know to escalate.

problems.title: Task assigned to wrong person
problems.text: Terrace waterproofing goes to the wrong trade. Issue appears after work starts.

alignment.title: Assigned tasks with clear accountability
alignment.text: Each task has owner, deadline, and status. Responsibilities are explicit.

alignment.title: Unified calendar with conflict detection
alignment.text: All visits, work slots, and deadlines in one shared schedule with automatic conflict checks.

alignment.title: Contextual communication
alignment.text: Messages are linked to tasks and documents so critical information is never lost.

alignment.title: Active notifications for every stakeholder
alignment.text: Each role receives only relevant updates. No one can claim they did not know.

alignment.title: Role-based visibility
alignment.text: Everyone sees only what matters for their role in that worksite context.

alignment.title: Shared worksite feed
alignment.text: One chronological feed for what happens on site. Photos, updates, approvals: aligned by default.

teamCoordinationPage.citation: Fragmented communication costs the construction industry $31B per year. Not because people do not want to communicate, but because they lack the right tools.
teamCoordinationPage.finalTitle: Ready to bring order to your construction site?
teamCoordinationPage.finalText: Stop wasting time across chats, emails, and calls. Start with EdilSync today and feel the difference from day one.
teamCoordinationPage.finalNote: Personal or company account - No card required to start - Upgrade when needed

privacyPolicyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicyPage.seoDescription: Privacy policy describing how personal data is processed on EdilSync.
privacyPolicyPage.intro: This policy explains how personal data is processed on EdilSync.
privacyPolicyPage.k2: The controller of personal data is EdilSync, reachable at:
privacyPolicyPage.k4: We collect the following categories of personal data:
privacyPolicyPage.k8: company name, VAT number, office address, phone
privacyPolicyPage.k10: access logs, interactions with the application
privacyPolicyPage.k12: documents, photos, messages uploaded by the user
privacyPolicyPage.k14: Data is processed for the following purposes:
privacyPolicyPage.k24: Personal data is retained for the duration of the contractual relationship and for the following 10 years for fiscal and legal compliance. Usage data is retained for 24 months.
privacyPolicyPage.k27: Other worksite participants (name, email, role) — necessary for service operation
privacyPolicyPage.k28: Technical service providers (hosting, email) — as processors
privacyPolicyPage.k29: Data is not sold to third parties.
privacyPolicyPage.k31: Under Articles 15-22 GDPR, users have the right to:
privacyPolicyPage.k34: Delete personal data ("right to be forgotten")
privacyPolicyPage.k39: Lodge a complaint with the Data Protection Authority
privacyPolicyPage.k40: To exercise your rights, write to:
privacyPolicyPage.k42: For information about cookie usage, see our
privacyPolicyPage.k44: We reserve the right to update this policy. Any changes will be communicated through the application.

termsOfServicePage.title: Terms of Service
termsOfServicePage.seoDescription: Terms and conditions that govern the use of the EdilSync platform.
termsOfServicePage.intro: These terms govern the use of the EdilSync platform.
termsOfServicePage.k2: EdilSync is a digital platform for managing construction worksites, enabling collaboration among clients, companies, professionals and consultants. The service includes worksite, task, document, messaging and calendar management.
termsOfServicePage.k4: Accessing and using EdilSync implies full acceptance of these Terms and Conditions. If you do not accept these terms, you may not use the service.
termsOfServicePage.k6: Users must provide truthful and updated information
termsOfServicePage.k7: Users are responsible for credential confidentiality
termsOfServicePage.k8: Each account is personal and non-transferable
termsOfServicePage.k9: Users are responsible for all activity performed through their account
termsOfServicePage.k12: Use the service in compliance with Italian and EU law
termsOfServicePage.k13: Not upload illegal, defamatory or rights-infringing content
termsOfServicePage.k14: Not attempt unauthorized access to data or features
termsOfServicePage.k15: Not use the service for purposes other than construction worksite management
termsOfServicePage.k17: The software, design, logos and all EdilSync content are protected by copyright and remain the exclusive property of the owner. Users retain ownership of content uploaded to the platform.
termsOfServicePage.k19: Users grant EdilSync a limited, non-exclusive license to host, display and transmit uploaded content solely for service delivery. Content shared within a worksite is visible to other worksite participants.
termsOfServicePage.k21: EdilSync is provided "as is". The owner does not guarantee the service is error-free or uninterrupted. In no case shall the owner be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from service use.
termsOfServicePage.k23: The owner reserves the right to suspend or terminate service access in case of terms violations, without prior notice. Users can delete their account at any time from settings.
termsOfServicePage.k25: These terms are governed by Italian law. Any dispute shall fall under the jurisdiction of the Court of [registered office], unless mandatory consumer protection rules provide otherwise.
termsOfServicePage.k27: We reserve the right to modify these terms. Changes will be communicated through the application with reasonable notice. Continued use after changes implies acceptance of the updated terms.
termsOfServicePage.k29: For questions related to these terms, write to:

cookiePolicyPage.title: Cookie Policy
cookiePolicyPage.seoDescription: Cookie policy for service operation and improvement on EdilSync.
cookiePolicyPage.intro: This policy explains how cookies are used for service operation and improvement.
cookiePolicyPage.k2: Cookies are small text files stored on the user's device while browsing. They are used to improve user experience and enable service operation.
cookiePolicyPage.k5: These cookies are essential for the app to function and cannot be disabled. They include:
cookiePolicyPage.k11: legitimate interest (Art. 6.1.f GDPR) — necessary for service delivery.
cookiePolicyPage.k13: We use analytics cookies to understand how users interact with the application and improve the service. These cookies collect aggregated and anonymous information.
cookiePolicyPage.k17: The app may include third-party services that set their own cookies. EdilSync has no control over those cookies. Please refer to the respective policies.
cookiePolicyPage.k19: Users can manage cookie preferences through:
cookiePolicyPage.k20: The cookie banner shown at first access
cookiePolicyPage.k22: Disabling technical cookies may affect application functionality.
cookiePolicyPage.k33: For more information on data-processing rights, see our
